summary: Completed testing of Google Auth integration for Bookvia. All backend and frontend tests passed. Google login button correctly appears only for regular users (not businesses). Backend endpoint properly validates session_id. Normal email login flows work without regression.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/auth/google/session returns 401 for invalid session_id - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/auth/google/session returns 400 for missing session_id - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/auth/google/session returns 400 for empty session_id - PASSED
Backend: Normal user login (cliente@bookvia.com) still works - PASSED
Backend: Normal business login (testrealstripe@bookvia.com) still works - PASSED
Backend: Health endpoint returns healthy - PASSED
Frontend: Google login button visible on /login page in 'Usuario' tab - PASSED
Frontend: Google login button NOT visible on /login page in 'Negocio' tab - PASSED
Frontend: Google register button visible on /register page - PASSED
Frontend: Google button NOT visible on /business/register page - PASSED
Frontend: /auth/google/callback route exists and redirects to /login when no session_id - PASSED
Frontend: Normal user email login works (redirects to home with '¡Bienvenido!' toast) - PASSED
Frontend: Normal business email login works (redirects to /business/dashboard) - PASSED
critical_code_review_comments: Google Auth implementation follows Emergent Auth flow correctly (auth.emergentagent.com)
LoginPage.jsx correctly shows Google button only when loginMode === 'user' (line 187)
RegisterPage.jsx has Google button for user registration (line 143)
BusinessRegisterPage.jsx correctly does NOT have Google button (business-only registration)
GoogleAuthCallback.jsx properly extracts session_id from URL hash and calls googleLogin
Backend /api/auth/google/session endpoint validates session_id with Emergent Auth API
Backend creates new user or updates existing user based on Google email
JWT token is properly generated after successful Google auth
window.location.origin is used dynamically for redirect URL (no hardcoding)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tested Google Auth integration: 1) POST /api/auth/google/session endpoint validates session_id (401 for invalid, 400 for missing), 2) Google button appears only for regular users on /login and /register pages, 3) Google button does NOT appear on /business/register or business login tab, 4) /auth/google/callback route exists and handles redirect, 5) Normal email login flows work without regression. The actual Google OAuth flow cannot be fully tested end-to-end since it requires real Google login through auth.emergentagent.com.

mocked_apis.note: Google Auth uses real Emergent Auth proxy (auth.emergentagent.com)

google_auth_backend.returns: JWT token + user data on success
google_auth_backend.error_handling: 401 for invalid session, 400 for missing session_id
